---
title: "FW 536 — Day 3 afternoon lab (v3 · aquatic & fisheries) — Your Name"
author: "Your Name Here"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: cosmo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# Day 3 afternoon lab — Model selection problem set

**Problem set version:** **v3 — aquatic & fisheries theme**.
(The companion version is [v2 — terrestrial wildlife](problem_set_v2.html); you choose whichever theme fits your interests.)

This template matches [`problem_set_v3.html`](problem_set_v3.html). Read each problem statement there, write your work below, **Knit** to HTML, and upload the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.

For setup help see the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html).

---

# Setup

```{r}
# Load packages you'll need. Add or remove as appropriate.
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(lme4)
library(nlme)
library(AICcmodavg)
library(MuMIn)
```

---

# Problem 6 — Bull trout occupancy: testing a random effect with LRT

> You fit two GLMMs of bull trout occupancy in 120 streams:

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 7 — Dungeness crab pot CPUE — full-subsets selection on 10 predictors

> You have 240 pot soaks with CPUE (count per pot per soak) and 10 candidate predictors: depth, substrate, distance-to-shore, kelp density, soak duration, bait type (5-level factor), and a few interactions. A colleague proposes running dredge() on a Poisson GLM and reporting the...

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 8 — Coastal cutthroat smolt-to-adult return rate

> You want to test 3 predictors of SAR: ocean_temp_anomaly (strong known driver), juvenile_size (modest), parr_density (weak). $n = 35$ year-stream combinations.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 9 — Eulachon (smelt) abundance forecasting

> You have 18 years of eulachon escapement counts in the Columbia, with annual covariates: spring_outflow, SST, NPGO, predator_index. The agency wants a model to predict next year's run.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 10 — Pacific halibut size-at-age and the inference question

> A halibut age/length dataset is fit with three competing candidate models: M1 has a fixed temperature effect, M2 has a fixed regional effect, M3 has both. AICc table:

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# (Optional) Embed a photo of hand-written work

For problems easier to do on paper, photograph and embed:

```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE, out.width="60%", fig.cap="Hand-derivation for Problem N"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/problemN_derivation.jpg")
```

(Change `eval=FALSE` to `eval=TRUE` after saving an image. See the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html) for details.)

---

# Submission checklist

- [ ] Answered every afternoon problem from `problem_set_v3.html`.
- [ ] Knitted to HTML without errors.
- [ ] Photos (if any) embedded with `self_contained: true` in the YAML.
- [ ] Uploaded the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.
